title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Jules, Gwendolyn, and Miss Marple discuss three potential suspects in the murder of Helen Haridy. 

The first suspect is Erskine. Jules reconstructs a theory where Erskine, who had an attractive quality toward women, met Helen on the beach. He may have insisted she run away with him, but when she refused, he strangled her in a rage. Jules suggests Erskine then buried the body and attempted to frame Kelvin Haridy. This theory accounts for the fact that Helen had expressed fear of a man and that Erskine admitted to seeing her again.

The second suspect is Walter Feen. Gwendolyn describes Feen as a repressed, obsessive man who was deeply hurt when Helen abandoned him in India to marry Kelvin Haridy. Although he appeared gentle, he had a history of violent rage as a child. Helen had reportedly told him, "I've always been afraid of you," and planned to move to Norfolk to escape him. Jules notes that Feen lived within walking distance of the scene and suggests he might have killed her if she changed her mind about running away with him, potentially to avoid a scandal.

The third suspect is Jackie Averycliff. Gwendolyn notes that Averycliff suffered from paranoia and held a grudge against Helen after a failed fling. He blamed Feen for losing his job. Jules posits that Averycliff may have been Helen's secret lover. This theory is supported by Lily Kimball’s letter mentioning a fancy car parked outside that night, which was identified as Averycliff's. Jules suggests that if Helen decided not to run away with him, Averycliff might have killed her and taken her suitcase and letters to make it appear she had fled abroad.
